description: You are setting up a new monitoring console at Svalbard Satellite Station (SvalSat). Configure a ground station named 'SvalSat' (78.2298 N, 15.4078 E, 450 meters altitude) and a tracking module named 'Dense_Constellation' assigned to it. The module must track these seven satellites: ISS (ZARYA) [NORAD 25544], CSS (TIANHE) [NORAD 48274], CSS (WENTIAN) [NORAD 53239], SUOMI NPP [NORAD 37849], FENGYUN 3A [NORAD 32958], FENGYUN 3B [NORAD 37214], and DMSP 5D-3 F18 (USA 210) [NORAD 35951]. Adjust the 'Dense_Constellation' module's map settings to disable both 'Coverage areas' and 'Ground tracks' so only the satellite markers remain visible. Set the global geographic coordinate format to Degrees, Minutes, Seconds (DMS). Login: username 'ga', password 'password123'.

natural_language.prompt: I'm setting up a tracking console at the Svalbard Satellite Station and I need your help configuring GPredict. First, add our 'SvalSat' ground station at 78.2298 North, 15.4078 East, and 450 meters altitude. Next, create a new tracking module called 'Dense_Constellation' and bind it to use the new SvalSat ground station. We need to track 7 specific satellites in this module: ISS ZARYA (25544), CSS TIANHE (48274), CSS WENTIAN (53239), SUOMI NPP (37849), FENGYUN 3A (32958), FENGYUN 3B (37214), and DMSP 5D-3 F18 (35951). Because there are so many, the map will be an unreadable mess of overlapping circles and lines. Please go into the module's map configuration and turn off both the Coverage Areas and the Ground Tracks to declutter the map. Lastly, please change the global coordinate format in the preferences to use Degrees, Minutes, Seconds (DMS).
